query: Evidence that radio waves from radio towers or car (mobile) phones affect brain cancer occurrence.
instruction: Persons living near radio towers and more recently persons using car (mobile) phones have been diagnosed with brain cancer. The argument rages regarding the direct association of one with the other. The incidence of cancer among the groups cited is considered, by some, to be higher than that found in the normal population. A relevant document includes any experiment with animals, statistical study, articles, news items which report on the incidence of brain cancer being higher/lower/same as those persons who live near a radio tower and those using car (mobile) phones as compared to those in the general population. Any mentions of leukemia is not relevant.
positive_anchors: Radio frequency radiation exposure from cellular towers and mobile devices linked to brain tumor development
Epidemiological studies and clinical trials on electromagnetic field effects on brain cancer rates
Comparative analysis of cancer incidence among populations near radio transmission equipment versus general population
discriminative_features: Must distinguish between brain cancer (glioma, meningioma, acoustic neuroma) and other cancers; focus on radio waves and electromagnetic fields not ionizing radiation or chemical carcinogens

query: A relevant document would discuss the frequency of vicious black bear attacks worldwide and the possible causes for this savage behavior.
instruction: It has been reported that food or cosmetics sometimes attract hungry black bears, causing them to viciously attack humans. Relevant documents would include the aforementioned causes (and must discuss trash) as well as speculation preferably from the scientific community as to other possible causes of vicious attacks by black bears. A relevant document would also detail steps taken or new methods devised by wildlife officials to control and/or modify the savageness of the black bear.
positive_anchors: Trash and food waste attraction as primary cause of black bear attacks on humans
Scientific studies on bear behavior and factors triggering aggressive responses
Wildlife management strategies and deterrent methods for controlling bear aggression
discriminative_features: Must focus on black bears specifically not grizzly or brown bears; emphasize trash/food attraction as required; distinguish between defensive and predatory attacks

query: A relevant document would discuss the effectiveness of efforts to better scrutinize passengers and luggage on all flights, but particularly international flights.
instruction: A relevant document would contain reports on what steps airports worldwide have taken to better scrutinize passengers and their luggage on domestic and international flights and to step up screening of all carry-on baggage and referencing terrorism. With increased concerns about terrorism, articles on airport security relating in particular to additional steps taken by airports to increase flight safety would be relevant. The mere mention of enhanced security does not constitute relevance. Additional steps refer to something beyond just passenger and carry-on screening using the normal methods. Examples of new steps would be additional personnel, automated screening processes, sophisticated monitoring and screening devices, whole body imaging techniques, and extraordinary measures to screen luggage in the baggage compartment.
positive_anchors: Advanced passenger screening technologies including whole body imaging and automated detection systems
Enhanced luggage screening methods and sophisticated monitoring devices for baggage compartments
Implementation of additional security personnel and extraordinary screening measures at airports worldwide
negative_constraints: mere mention of enhanced security without specifics
discriminative_features: Must specify new technologies or methods beyond standard screening; must reference terrorism context; distinguish between normal procedures and extraordinary measures

query: The availability of E-mail to many people through their job or school affiliation has allowed for many efficiencies in communications but also has provided the opportunity for abuses. What steps have been taken world-wide by those bearing the cost of E-mail to prevent excesses?
instruction: To be relevant, a document will concern dissatisfaction by an entity paying for the cost of electronic mail as well as describe issues of surveillance. Particularly sought are items which relate to system users (such as employees) who abuse the system by engaging in communications of the type not related to the payer's desired use of the system.
positive_anchors: Organizational monitoring and surveillance of employee email communications
Policies and measures implemented by employers to prevent email system abuse
Costs and financial burdens of email systems borne by organizations and institutions
discriminative_features: Must involve organizational perspective not individual user concerns; must include surveillance or monitoring aspects; focus on abuse prevention not general email usage policies

query: Identify documents discussing the development and application of space-borne ocean remote sensing.
instruction: Documents discussing the development and application of space-borne ocean remote sensing in oceanography, seabed prospecting and mining, or any marine-science activity are relevant. Documents that discuss the application of satellite remote sensing in geography, agriculture, forestry, mining and mineral prospecting or any land-bound science are not relevant, nor are references to international marketing or promotional advertising of any remote-sensing technology. Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) employed in ocean remote sensing is relevant. Information about temperature is not relevant.
positive_anchors: Satellite-based ocean observation systems for marine science and oceanography
Space-borne synthetic aperture radar SAR applications in seabed exploration and ocean monitoring
Remote sensing technologies for marine resource assessment and underwater prospecting
discriminative_features: Must focus on ocean/marine applications not land-based; exclude temperature information; distinguish between scientific applications and commercial marketing

query: Identify documents discussing the use of estrogen by postmenopausal women in Britain.
instruction: The use of hormone replacement therapy outside of the United Kingdom is not relevant. United Kingdom and British development and marketing of estrogen suppressing drugs are relevant (but ignore any documents mentioning testosterone).
positive_anchors: Hormone replacement therapy usage among postmenopausal women in the United Kingdom
British development and clinical application of estrogen-related treatments
UK healthcare policies and medical practices regarding menopausal hormone therapy
discriminative_features: Must be UK-specific not general international studies; focus on estrogen not testosterone; include both therapy usage and drug development contexts

query: What modern instances have there been of old fashioned piracy, the boarding or taking control of boats?
instruction: Documents discussing piracy on any body of water and terrorism are relevant. Documents discussing the legal taking of ships or their contents by a national authority are non-relevant. Clashes between fishing vessels over fishing are not relevant, unless one vessel is boarded.
positive_anchors: Modern maritime piracy incidents involving vessel boarding and hijacking
Pirate attacks on commercial shipping and private boats in international waters
Terrorism-related maritime violence and vessel seizures by non-state actors
negative_constraints: legal ship seizure by coast guard
discriminative_features: Must involve illegal boarding not legal authority actions; distinguish piracy from fishing conflicts; include terrorism connection when present

query: Identify documents that discuss mainstreaming children with physical or mental impairments.
instruction: A relevant document will include the pros and cons of mainstreaming children with physical or mental impairments, the benefits to the impaired child, as well as the attitude, beliefs and concerns of teachers and school administrators with regard to taking time away from the normal children. The document must quote from someone from the state or federal government to be authoritative.
positive_anchors: Inclusion education benefits and challenges for children with disabilities in regular classrooms
Teacher and administrator perspectives on special needs student integration
Government policy statements on mainstreaming and inclusive education practices
discriminative_features: Must include government official quotes for authority; cover both benefits and concerns; focus on mainstreaming not special education segregation

query: What measures are being taken by local South American authorities to preserve the Amazon tropical rain forest?
instruction: Relevant documents may identify: the official organizations, institutions, and individuals of the countries included in the Amazon rain forest; the measures being taken by them to preserve the rain forest; and indications of degrees of success in these endeavors. Exclude any mentions of native Indians.
positive_anchors: Brazilian and South American government conservation programs for Amazon rainforest protection
Environmental policies and regulatory measures implemented by Amazon basin countries
Success metrics and effectiveness assessments of rainforest preservation initiatives
discriminative_features: Must focus on government authorities not indigenous populations; emphasize preservation measures and their effectiveness; cover multiple Amazon basin countries

query: How often were the peace talks in Ireland delayed or disrupted as a result of acts of violence?
instruction: Any interruptions to the peace process not directly attributable to acts of violence are not relevant. The violence must have happened primarily in Ireland, not specifically England.
positive_anchors: Violent incidents causing delays in Northern Ireland peace negotiations
Terrorist attacks and paramilitary violence disrupting Irish peace process
Timeline of violence-related interruptions to Good Friday Agreement talks
discriminative_features: Must link disruptions specifically to violent acts not political disagreements; violence must occur in Ireland not England; focus on peace talks timeline

query: How much sugar does Cuba export and which countries import it?
instruction: A relevant document will provide information regarding Cuba's sugar trade and Fidel Castro. Sugar production statistics are not relevant unless exports are mentioned explicitly.
positive_anchors: Cuba's sugar export volumes and trade statistics to importing nations
International sugar trade partnerships and commercial agreements involving Cuba
Fidel Castro's role and policies in Cuban sugar export industry
negative_constraints: sugar cane cultivation data without export context
discriminative_features: Must explicitly mention exports not just production; must include Fidel Castro reference; focus on trade relationships not agricultural statistics

query: What is the ongoing status of The Three Gorges Project?
instruction: A relevant document will provide the projected or actual date of completion of the project, its estimated or actual total cost, or the estimated or ongoing electrical output of the finished project. Discussions of the social, political, or ecological impact of the project are not relevant. Discussion of the displacement of peoples for this project is extra not relevant, do not return any that talk about that.
positive_anchors: Three Gorges Dam construction timeline and projected completion dates
Financial costs and budget estimates for the Three Gorges hydroelectric project
Power generation capacity and electrical output statistics of the completed dam
discriminative_features: Must focus on technical project status not social consequences; exclude displacement and resettlement topics; emphasize construction progress and power output

query: What new uses have been developed for old automobile tires as a means of tire recycling?
instruction: A relevant document must show advantageous uses of recycled tires, such as: destructive distillation of scrap rubber for valuable chemicals, reef building for fish habitats, filler or binder in asphalt roadway mixes, and burning in a controlled environment for heat generation. Exclude any references of rubber.
positive_anchors: Destructive distillation of scrap tires to extract valuable chemical compounds
Artificial reef construction using discarded tires for marine habitat creation
Asphalt road paving applications utilizing tire-derived filler and binder materials
Controlled combustion of waste tires for industrial heat and energy generation
discriminative_features: Must focus on tire recycling applications not rubber material science; exclude rubber industry content; emphasize end-use applications of recycled tires

query: Is there contemporary interest in the Greek philosophy of stoicism?
instruction: Actual references to the philosophy or philosophers in books or by professors counts as interest while productions of Greek stoic plays and new stoic artistic productions are all irrelevant (as are descriptions of modern people in respect to stoics).
positive_anchors: Academic scholarly works and philosophical texts on Greek Stoicism
University professors and philosophy experts discussing Stoic doctrine
Contemporary philosophical interest in Stoic ethics and practical wisdom
discriminative_features: Must be academic philosophical discussion not artistic productions; exclude modern character descriptions; focus on scholarly analysis of Stoic philosophy

query: What measures have been taken worldwide and what countries have been effective in curbing population growth?
instruction: A relevant document must describe an actual case in which population measures have been taken and their results are known and from China. The reduction measures must have been actively pursued; that is, passive events such as disease or famine involuntarily reducing the population are not relevant.
positive_anchors: China's one-child policy and family planning implementation results
Active government-sponsored population control programs and their effectiveness
Policy interventions and birth rate reduction strategies in various countries
discriminative_features: Must focus on China specifically; emphasize active policy measures not passive events; distinguish intentional programs from natural demographic changes

query: What other countries besides the United States are considering or have approved women as clergy persons?
instruction: To be relevant, a document must indicate either a country where a woman has been installed as clergy or a country that is considering such an installation. The clergy position must be as church pastor rather than some other church capacity (e.g., nun or choir member). Please do not return any document that has references to American politics, war, or churches, even if it does contain other countries (as I am looking outside of the US).
positive_anchors: International countries ordaining women as church pastors and clergy leaders
Global religious institutions approving female pastoral leadership roles
National policies and denominational decisions on women in clergy positions
discriminative_features: Must be non-US countries only; focus on pastor role not other church positions; exclude American political context even when mentioning other nations

query: Find information about the first genetically modified food product to go on the market, Flavr Savr (also Flavor Saver) Tomato developed by Calgene.
instruction: Documents about genetically engineered food in general are not relevant; relevant documents must not include specifics regarding the Flavr Savr tomato.
q_plus: First genetically modified food product to go on the market, the Flavr Savr tomato developed by Calgene
discriminative_features: Must exclude Flavr Savr tomato details; exclude general GMO food discussions; this is a negative constraint query where the topic itself is the exclusion

query: How did France respond to protests against its nuclear testing in the South Pacific?
instruction: A document containing information regarding any type of protest by any one is relevant as long as France's response (attributable to Chirac himself, not generic France) is also included in the document.
positive_anchors: President Jacques Chirac's personal statements on French nuclear testing policy
French government official response to South Pacific protest movements
Chirac's diplomatic reactions and policy decisions regarding nuclear test opposition
discriminative_features: Must attribute response specifically to Chirac not general French government; require direct quotes or clear attribution to Jacques Chirac; exclude anonymous official statements

query: What efforts have been made to stabilize the Leaning Tower of Pisa, and how successful have the efforts been?
instruction: Relevant documents provide discussions of the current condition of the tower, describe reinforcement measures taken, and/or provide measurements reflecting change in the tower. Exclude documents mentioning the year 1990.
positive_anchors: Engineering stabilization interventions for the Leaning Tower of Pisa tilt correction
Structural reinforcement techniques and foundation stabilization methods applied
Quantitative measurements and monitoring data on tower inclination changes
discriminative_features: Must exclude any 1990 references; focus on stabilization efforts not general history; emphasize technical measurements and current condition assessments

query: Find documents which describe an advantage in hiring potential or increased income for graduates of U.S. colleges.
instruction: Relevant documents cite some advantage of a college education for job opportunities. Documents citing better opportunities for non-college vocational-training is not relevant. I do not trust colleges in Manhattan as they have a different economy, so find documents that do not cite or discuss these colleges (e.g. Columbia, etc.).
positive_anchors: Employment advantages and career benefits for United States college graduates
Income differentials and salary premiums associated with college degrees
Job market opportunities and hiring preferences for university-educated workers
discriminative_features: Must be US college graduates not vocational training; exclude Manhattan and NYC institutions specifically; focus on employment and income advantages
